evals.name: pricing-page-nextjs
evals.prompt: clone this pricing page https://posthog.com/pricing into a fresh next.js + tailwind project. focus on the hero + pricing cards section. dont need backend, just static UI. write the component file(s) and any global css needed.
evals.expected_output: A Next.js page component (.tsx) using Tailwind, with the hero section + pricing cards layout matching the source, including responsive breakpoints

evals.name: linear-hero-plain
evals.prompt: recreate https://www.linear.app homepage hero in plain HTML+CSS, no framework. make sure it works on mobile too — the gradient bg and the floating shapes are important to match. write index.html and styles.css.
evals.expected_output: An index.html + styles.css implementing the Linear hero, with gradient background, floating shapes, responsive mobile breakpoint

evals.name: github-nav-react
evals.prompt: i love the navigation bar on https://github.com/anthropics/skills (the top GitHub nav with the search bar, notifications, profile menu). build just that nav as a react component with tailwind, with the search input + buttons. one file, default export the component.
evals.expected_output: A single .tsx file exporting a React component for the GitHub-style nav, using Tailwind, with search input + button placeholders
